reviews.review_msg: He is kind, reasonable and knowledgeable. He relate material to real world issues. Often tell us some important news in real world and give us his insight. He also have extensive insight in historical economic theory, which help me understand theory better. He also teach extra material that help students who want to go to graduate school. very kind

Harris is the most boring prof you will ever have. He is unenthoused and monotone. His exams are extremely easy though. The midterm for econ 105 was all multiple choice and some of the questions were so easy they made me laugh. You'll have to teach yourself mainly from the textbook, but you'll probably get a decent mark at least.
he can be boring, but i found him decend enough to get me interested in the course. exams are fully based on textbook, good prof :)

Clearly knows his material, although most of what's explained in lecture is never tested. Exams and midterms are stolen off of textbook website, and makes very little effort to answer any emails or clarify things. Boring as hell, lectures are not actually mandatory unless you want a 10% clicker grade.

This class was about financial crisis and it was pretty fun class. Materials were helpful and prof has a lot of knowledge about financial crisis too. He just does not email you back. One midterm was easy and another midterm was relatively hard and final was pretty fair. I've learned a lot from this class.

I feel bad for the people who took him in lower division classes, I cant see this being his thing at all. As for 403, this is DEFINITELY his interest area. Tons of knowledge and understanding, and makes things make sense, textbook theory he is not so keen on  but once he starts talking about something hes interested in...phenomenal!
Why does he teach if he hates students so much?  Gives a bad impression of the Econ department.
He doesn't seem interested in teaching at all. The lectures are very scattered. I wouldn't recommend taking a class with him.

Very good prof.Interesting, and surprisingly, can be funny. Had little knowledge and interest in the subject before i took the course, now I love it. Midterms and final were fair, make sure you read the book. Would def recommend taking this course and having this prof.
Awesome prof. Top academic, too. Pleasure to be in his class.

Worst course I have ever taken. Prof Harris knows his stuff, and is very smart, but he should stick to research not teaching! His lecture was scattered, had little or no relevance to what was on exams AND textbooks. Course outline is very unorganized and confusing. Notes and midterms are full of typos and mistakes. Feel sorry for the TAs.
one swear word could describe him and his econ class

I can honestly say that he's a pretty bad professor. He is a very smart man, with a tremendous amount of economic knowledge, but he doesn't seem to be interested in teaching at all. His exams are ridiculously hard.
boring and mean. Acts like a "know it all"

Regardless of what you've been told this is NOT an automatic GPA booster class. Dr Harris is very dull and doesn't seem interested in the course. His mid term and final are too hard for a 100 level. The only positive is that he puts all of his notes online.
Worst Prof Ever!
No Comments
Very interesting material, but he doesn't teach it with much enthusiasm...  Sexy for his age group. (look at his pic on the Department website)... Memorize your notes.
His lectures are extremely boring.  However, the course is interesting and easy itself.  Just study the notes and read the book.  You'll end up doing well.
look up the word boring in the dicitonary and you might find his name.
Easy econ course becuz its 100 level. Alot easier compare to Econ 103/105. Mostly interesting concepts and not boring concepts like 103/105. Just read/memerize all notes for sure will get at least B.
His course material is very easy. IF u study and memorize the lecture notes u will be fine. TAs are cool too and easy markers but they gotta learn how to speak some english.

The lecture topics are very interesting! I like how he structured the course. A bit of each of economic history, globalization and current world issues. You can find some spelling and grammar mistakes in his notes. But that is tolerable. Overall, he is a good prof who knows his stuff...
Really good professor.  Interesting and intellectual lectures
Stick to research.
His lectures are very inersting and obviously he knows what he is doing in the class. If u want to get a job in government office, his course is a must.

The worst teacher I have ever seem in my lifetime.Mean,confusing,mumbling,hostile.This man clearly hates teaching,so why do it and torture us?Should stay in his office and do research.Can't believe the dept. lets him teach.

A brilliant professor! His depth of knowledge and high interest on the subject is evident in the breadth of topics covered in class. Although lectures can be a bit dry, a class with Harris is a must if knowledge is what you seek.
Very smart teacher! Yet, don't know how to teach and expect you to know things that ain't taught.
